title: The Great Gatsby
author: F. Scott Fitzgerald
description: A classic story of ambition, wealth, and the elusive American Dream set in the Roaring Twenties.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: A handbook of agile software craftsmanship, teaching how to write code that is readable and maintainable.

title: A Brief History of Time
author: Stephen Hawking
description: A landmark volume in science writing that explores the origins and eventual fate of the universe.

title: The Hobbit
author: J.R.R. Tolkien
description: An adventurous story of Bilbo Baggins as he journeys to reclaim a lost treasure from a dragon.

title: Eloquent JavaScript
author: Marijn Haverbeke
description: A modern introduction to programming and the JavaScript language, perfect for web developers.

title: Cosmos
author: Carl Sagan
description: An exploration of 15 billion years of cosmic evolution and the development of science and civilization.

author: George Orwell
description: A dystopian story about total surveillance and the loss of individual freedom in a future society.

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
description: Essential lessons for modern developers, covering everything from personal responsibility to architectural techniques.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of humankind, exploring how biology and history have defined us as a species.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird
author: Harper Lee
description: A story of innocence and injustice in the Deep South, told through the eyes of young Scout Finch.

title: You Don't Know JS Yet
author: Kyle Simpson
description: A deep dive into the core mechanics of the JavaScript language, focusing on scope and closures.

title: The Selfish Gene
author: Richard Dawkins
description: A revolutionary book that explains the theory of evolution from the perspective of the gene.
